MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF ABRAHAM DEGALA'S Motion To Compel Defendant The John Stewart Company To Comply With Document Requests.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion is GRANTED. Defendant John Stewart Co. shall serve code-compliant verified responses to Plaintiff's request for production of documents (set three) within fourteen (14) days of the date of this Order without objection including claims of privilege and work product. The term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions against Defendant and its attorney of record Shelley Kramer is GRANTED in the amount of $500, said sum to be paid by November 20, 2024.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to pvestal@nvlawllp.com.
